journal_entry.rationale: Utility services consumed but not yet paid represent an expense in the current period and a liability (accounts payable). The debit increases Utilities Expense (expense increase), and the credit increases Accounts Payable (liability increase). Utilities are typically exempt from HST/GST in Canada, so no tax lines are required.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense now even though payment hasn't been made yet. The credit side records the liability increase (accounts payable/accrued expenses) for the amount owed to the utility company.

output.reason: Entry correctly accrues $50 utility expense. Debit to Utilities Expense (expense increase) and credit to Accounts Payable (liability increase) are both directionally correct. Entry balances (50 debit = 50 credit). No tax required as utilities are typically exempt from HST/GST in Canada. All necessary lines present.
